scenes.speech: Don't write the same code twice! Learn the magic of functions!
scenes.title: Functions

scenes.speech: A mechanism to bundle and reuse code!
scenes.title: Basic Functions
scenes.content: def greet(name): print('Hello, '+name) defines a function. Call it as many times as you want!

scenes.speech: Pass values in, get results out!
scenes.title: Parameters and Return Values
scenes.content: def add(a, b): return a + b → result = add(3, 5) → result = 8. Params = input, return = output!

scenes.title: Default Arguments
scenes.content: def greet(name='Guest'): works even without arguments. greet() → 'Hello, Guest'

scenes.title: Keyword Arguments
scenes.content: greet(name='Alice') specifies the argument by name. Safe even if you mix up the order!

scenes.title: Variable Arguments (*args)
scenes.content: def total(*args): return sum(args) lets total(1,2,3,4,5) work with any number of args!

scenes.speech: The range where variables can be used!
scenes.title: Scope
scenes.content: Variables inside a function can't be used outside (local scope). Variables outside are global scope!

scenes.speech: Short functions you can write in one line!
scenes.title: Lambda Functions
scenes.content: add = lambda x, y: x + y writes a simple function on one line. Often used with map and filter!

scenes.title: Function Design
scenes.content: One function does one thing, starts with a verb, stays short... code becomes readable and maintainable!
scenes.highlight: 1 function 1 job + clear name = good code

scenes.speech: You can now write clean, reusable code with functions!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
